skill.name: overdrive
skill.description: Guides agents to create ambitious browser interfaces with advanced animation, rendering, and performance techniques while preserving accessibility and fallbacks.
skill.summary: Design and implement high-impact frontend interactions with disciplined performance checks.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution, weak cryptographic algorithms, and system reconnaissance in SKILL.md. Manual review found Markdown formatting, frontend API names, CSS and JavaScript examples, and ordinary prose rather than executable commands or malicious behavior. No prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration, credential access, or unsafe system interaction was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The backtick block at the response banner is Markdown formatting for display text, not Ruby shell execution or a command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are a fenced Markdown banner with no shell command, interpreter call, or user-controlled execution path. The surrounding text only instructs response formatting.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Frontend Syntax Examples
low_findings.description: Inline backticks and fenced CSS or JavaScript examples describe browser animation APIs and fallback checks. They are documentation examples, not external command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain CSS feature checks, browser API names, and JavaScript capability detection. There is no evidence of shell execution, process spawning, or command construction.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The high-severity matches occur in descriptive prose and skill workflow guidance. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, system enumeration command, or reconnaissance behavior appears at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary English text and design instructions at these lines. The analyzer appears to have matched substrings rather than an actual cryptographic or reconnaissance implementation.

content.value_statement: Advanced frontend effects can easily become slow, distracting, or inaccessible. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code choose ambitious techniques that fit the product and remain usable.
actual_capabilities: Guides selection of advanced browser techniques for visual, functional, data-heavy, and performance-critical interfaces.
Requires design context before implementation so effects match the product audience and purpose.
Promotes progressive enhancement with static fallbacks for unsupported browser features.
Covers animation, WebGL, Canvas, Web Workers, WASM, virtual scrolling, and GPU-accelerated charts.
Adds performance and accessibility checks, including reduced motion support and mid-range device testing.
Encourages browser automation review and iterative visual refinement before shipping.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include reusable code, assets, or packaged components.
It depends on separate design context and may require another skill before implementation.
It can increase implementation complexity when a simple interface would be enough.
It does not replace product decisions such as collaboration, offline support, or backend features.

use_cases.title: Create a High-Impact Marketing Surface
use_cases.description: Plan scroll reveals, shader backgrounds, or cinematic transitions that fit a brand without weakening performance.

use_cases.title: Improve a Dense Product Workflow
use_cases.description: Make dialogs, forms, tables, and navigation feel faster through animation, optimistic feedback, and smooth state transitions.

use_cases.title: Optimize Data-Heavy Views
use_cases.description: Use virtual scrolling, worker processing, and GPU-rendered charts for large datasets that must remain responsive.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Interface Upgrade
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a simple UI and want a tasteful enhancement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the overdrive skill on this interface. Propose two or three fitting enhancement directions before coding, with performance and accessibility trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Motion System Planning
prompt_templates.scenario: You need coordinated animation across several components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply overdrive to design a motion approach for this product flow. Include fallbacks, reduced motion behavior, and browser support concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Large Dataset Interaction
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a table, chart, or search view to stay smooth at scale.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use overdrive to improve this data-heavy interface. Recommend rendering, worker, and virtualization techniques that preserve a responsive experience.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Visual Implementation Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You already built an ambitious effect and need a rigorous review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this overdrive implementation for visual quality, frame rate, progressive enhancement, reduced motion support, and unnecessary complexity.

output_examples.input: A SaaS dashboard needs to feel more polished without becoming distracting.
output_examples.output: A prioritized plan for subtle state transitions, instant save feedback, accessible reduced motion behavior, and performance checks for mid-range devices.

output_examples.input: A portfolio hero needs a memorable first impression.
output_examples.output: Three visual directions using scroll-driven reveals, pointer-responsive rendering, or a lightweight shader, each with fallback and browser support notes.

output_examples.input: A table must handle very large row counts smoothly.
output_examples.output: A technical approach using virtual scrolling, deferred measurement, worker-backed filtering, and visual verification of frame stability.

best_practices: Gather design context before choosing a technique or visual direction.
Use progressive enhancement and keep the fallback experience useful.
Verify the result in a browser with performance, device, and accessibility checks.
anti_patterns: Adding advanced effects because the API is new rather than because the product needs them.
Shipping motion or rendering work without reduced motion support and browser fallbacks.
Layering several competing effects that make the interface harder to understand.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build ambitious browser interfaces using animation, rendering, performance, and interaction techniques.

faq.question: Does it generate ready-made components?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and review criteria for the current project context.

faq.question: Is it only for marketing pages?
faq.answer: No. It also covers functional UI such as tables, forms, dialogs, dashboards, and search views.

faq.question: How does it handle accessibility?
faq.answer: It requires reduced motion support, graceful fallbacks, and checks that the interface remains usable.

faq.question: Can it use advanced browser APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It discusses WebGL, WebGPU, Canvas, workers, WASM, View Transitions, and scroll-driven animation.

faq.question: Why does it ask for user confirmation first?
faq.answer: Ambitious effects can misfit a product. The skill asks for direction before implementation to reduce wasted work.
